nativeRange.habitat: Shallow reef and rocky subtidal zones, typically in the photic zone

avoidWith: herbivorous fish that graze heavily on macroalgae (e.g. tangs, rabbitfish in small tanks)

imageCandidates.notes: Thomas Irvine, 2048x1463. In-situ underwater shot showing the wiry, irregularly branching thalli with vivid blue-green/gold structural iridescence among pink coralline algae - the diagnostic look of Coelothrix irregularis.
Thomas Irvine, 1024x1024. Close-up of the iridescent wiry turf; clearly shows green-gold iridescence but slightly soft, so kept as a non-recommended extra.
Thomas Irvine, 928x1024. Whole collected thallus in a tray showing the irregular wiry branching morphology; out-of-water and drab (no iridescence), included only to document overall structure.

summary: Blue Live Wire (Coelothrix irregularis) is a rare ornamental red macroalga with thin, wiry, irregularly branching thalli that display a vivid blue iridescence under aquarium lighting. Suited to display reef tanks with moderate light and stable reef water chemistry.
careNotes:
Blue Live Wire is a specialty ornamental red macroalga in the family Champiaceae, order Rhodymeniales. Its most distinctive trait is a vivid blue iridescence on the thin, wiry, irregularly branching thallus — a structural coloration effect typical of the genus Coelothrix. The species remains uncommon in the hobby and is available only through a small number of specialty online retailers; species-specific captive care literature is limited, and the guidance below is extrapolated largely from published care standards for red macroalgae in the Champiaceae family.

Standard reef water parameters are generally advised: temperature around 24–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, alkalinity 8–12 dKH, and specific gravity 1.023–1.026. Medium-intensity lighting tends to be sufficient. Growth rate is moderate for a macroalga, and nutrient uptake is relatively low compared to faster-growing genera. Placement in the display portion of the aquarium is recommended to make the most of the iridescent coloration.

As a red alga, Blue Live Wire should NEVER be buried in the substrate — contact with sand at the base will cause the tissue to melt. Instead, attach the thallus directly to rockwork using cyanoacrylate (superglue gel), zip-ties, fishing line, or by wedging it firmly into a crevice. Sand-bed placement is generally appropriate only for green macroalgae such as Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea, as well as some calcified Dasycladales; red and brown algae are typically rock-attached species.

Heavily herbivorous fish — tangs and rabbitfish in particular — may graze on macroalgae and are generally best avoided in smaller tanks where Blue Live Wire is being grown as a display specimen.
breedingNotes: Propagation in the aquarium is generally accomplished by fragmentation: healthy branch tips are broken off and then attached to rockwork or wedged into crevices, where they typically establish and continue growing. Sexual reproduction in red algae follows an alternating gametophyte–sporophyte lifecycle, but this process has not been documented in hobby aquaria for this species.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby-trade source for this species. Product listing for 'Iridescent Wire Algae' (Coelothrix irregularis). Provides identification, imagery, and general reef suitability context.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
